payload.source_text:
QUALITY POLICY STATEMENT
Novus Mechanical Services Limited has established a Quality Policy to be consistent with the purpose and context of our organisation.

It provides a framework for the setting and review of objectives in addition to our commitment to satisfy applicable customers’, regulatory and legislative requirements as well as our commitment to continually improve our management system.
Customer focus: As an organisation we have made a commitment to understand our current and future customers’ needs; meet their requirements and strive to exceed their expectations.
Leadership: Our Top Management have committed to creating and maintaining a working environment in which people become fully involved in achieving our objectives.
Engagement of people: As an organisation we recognise that people are the essence of any good business and that their full involvement enables their abilities to be used for our benefit.

Process approach: As an organisation we understand that a desired result is achieved more efficiently when activities and related resources are managed as a process or series of interconnected processes.
Improvement: We have committed to achieving continual improvement across all aspects of our quality management system; it is one of our main annual objectives.
Evidence-based decision making: As an organisation we have committed to only make decisions relating to our QMS following an analysis of relevant data and information.

Relationship management: We recognises that an organisation and the relationship it has with its external providers are interdependent and a mutually beneficial relationship enhances the ability of both to create value.
Our policy is also to meet the requirements of our social, environmental, charitable and legislative responsibilities.

Signed : 							Date: 22/4/2025
           	
Damian Shacklock
Director
NOVUS MECHANICAL SERVICES LTD.

payload.description: Quality objective-setting process
payload.justification: Objective-setting is a strategic process defined within the quality policy framework purpose.

payload.description: Continual quality improvement process
payload.justification: Continual improvement is both a framework purpose commitment and core organizational process executed annually.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making process
payload.justification: Evidence-based decision making is a strategic process governing quality management governance.

payload.description: Quality policy framework
payload.justification: Quality policy is a strategic entity that governs organizational quality management aligned with business context and purpose.

payload.description: Quality policy alignment with business purpose and context
payload.justification: Relationship between quality policy entity and organizational business purpose/context as strategic alignment.

payload.description: Customer needs and expectations
payload.justification: Customer needs and expectations are strategic entities that drive organizational commitment and value delivery.

payload.description: External provider relationships
payload.justification: External provider relationships are strategic entities recognized for value creation.

payload.description: Annual continual improvement cycle
payload.justification: Annual cycle is the strategic temporal framework for continual improvement organizational objective.

payload.description: Organizational customers
payload.justification: Customers are strategic stakeholders whose needs and expectations drive organizational commitment.

payload.description: Organizational people and workforce
payload.justification: People/workforce are strategic stakeholders whose engagement is recognized as essential organizational value.

payload.description: Organizational leadership
payload.justification: Leadership is a strategic actor committed to creating enabling work environment for objectives.

payload.description: External providers
payload.justification: External providers are strategic stakeholders in mutually beneficial value creation relationships.

payload.description: Organizational director authorizing role
payload.justification: Director role provides formal policy authorization establishing governance authority and accountability.

payload.description: Understanding and exceeding customer expectations
payload.justification: Strategic goal articulating organizational commitment to customer needs understanding and expectations exceeding.

payload.description: Achieving organizational objectives
payload.justification: Strategic goal for which leadership creates enabling work environment.

payload.description: Creating mutually beneficial value with external providers
payload.justification: Strategic goal driving external provider relationship management for value creation.

payload.description: People engagement as strategic enabler
payload.justification: Strategic principle recognizing people engagement as essential organizational value for business benefit.

payload.description: Process approach for organizational efficiency
payload.justification: Strategic principle declaring process approach as method for achieving desired results efficiently.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making for quality governance
payload.justification: Strategic principle committing organization to evidence-based decision making in quality management.

payload.description: Regulatory compliance requirement
payload.justification: Strategic constraint requiring organizational commitment to regulatory compliance within quality policy framework.

payload.description: Social responsibility obligations
payload.justification: Strategic constraint establishing organizational commitment to social responsibilities.

payload.description: Environmental responsibility obligations
payload.justification: Strategic constraint establishing organizational commitment to environmental responsibilities.

payload.description: Charitable responsibility obligations
payload.justification: Strategic constraint establishing organizational commitment to charitable responsibilities.

payload.description: Legislative compliance obligations
payload.justification: Strategic constraint establishing organizational commitment to legislative compliance responsibilities.

payload.description: Customer focus process encompassing needs understanding and expectation management
payload.justification: SG449 articulates customer focus as requiring understanding, meeting requirements, and exceeding expectations, describing a business process.

payload.description: Leadership process creating enabling environments for objective achievement
payload.justification: SG450 defines leadership as creating environments that enable achievement, representing a business process activity.

payload.description: People engagement process leveraging human capabilities
payload.justification: SG451 identifies people engagement as recognizing and utilizing human capabilities, describing a business process.

payload.description: Process approach managing activities and resources as interconnected system
payload.justification: SG452 explicitly establishes process approach as managing activities and resources, defining a core business process methodology.

payload.description: Continual improvement process across quality management system
payload.justification: SG453 defines continual improvement as an ongoing business process spanning all aspects of the QMS.

payload.description: Data analysis process supporting quality management decisions
payload.justification: SG454 establishes data analysis as a required activity before decisions, describing a business process.

payload.description: Relationship management process with external providers
payload.justification: SG455 defines relationship management as a conceptual model requiring active management, representing a business process.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making required before quality management system decisions
payload.justification: SG454 explicitly establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule requiring data analysis.

payload.description: Objective-setting function within policy framework
payload.justification: SG448 identifies objective-setting as a core function encompassed by the policy framework.

payload.description: Quality policy as a documented business entity
payload.justification: SG447 establishes quality policy as a conceptual foundation, indicating it is a business entity that exists within the organization's context.

payload.description: Customer requirement as a business data object
payload.justification: SG448 and SG449 both reference customer requirements as core elements that must be understood and met, representing a key business entity.

payload.description: Quality objective as a business data object
payload.justification: SG448 explicitly defines objectives as part of the policy framework, establishing them as business entities.

payload.description: Regulatory compliance requirement as a business entity
payload.justification: SG448 and SG456 identify regulatory and legislative requirements as formal entities within the policy scope.

payload.description: External provider relationship as a business entity
payload.justification: SG455 defines external provider relationships as a conceptual model, representing them as distinct business entities.

payload.description: Quality policy alignment with organizational purpose
payload.justification: SG447 establishes the relationship between quality policy and organizational purpose/context as a foundational connection.

payload.description: Process interconnection enabling system efficiency
payload.justification: SG452 explicitly describes processes as interconnected, defining a relationship model between business processes.

payload.description: Interdependency between organization and external providers
payload.justification: SG455 defines external provider relationships as interdependent, establishing a mutual relationship model.

payload.description: Customer as business actor with needs and expectations
payload.justification: SG449 explicitly identifies customers as actors whose needs must be understood and expectations exceeded.

payload.description: External provider as business actor in value creation relationship
payload.justification: SG455 defines external providers as actors in interdependent relationships that mutually create value.

payload.description: Leadership role responsible for creating enabling environments
payload.justification: SG450 defines leadership as a conceptual responsibility, representing a business role within the organization.

payload.description: People role contributing capabilities as organizational assets
payload.justification: SG451 identifies people as having capabilities essential to the organization, describing their role as business contributors.

payload.description: Meet customer requirements as business objective
payload.justification: SG449 identifies meeting customer requirements as a core business goal within the customer focus concept.

payload.description: Exceed customer expectations as business objective
payload.justification: SG449 explicitly includes exceeding expectations as a goal beyond merely meeting requirements.

payload.description: Enable objective achievement through people involvement
payload.justification: SG450 identifies enabling objective achievement as the business goal of leadership activities.

payload.description: Achieve efficiency through interconnected process management
payload.justification: SG452 establishes efficiency as the business goal of the process approach model.

payload.description: Continual improvement across all quality management system aspects
payload.justification: SG453 explicitly defines continual improvement as a central business objective.

payload.description: Mutual value creation through external provider relationships
payload.justification: SG455 identifies mutual value creation as the business goal of relationship management.

payload.description: Customer focus principle requiring understanding and exceeding expectations
payload.justification: SG449 articulates customer focus as a business concept representing a foundational principle.

payload.description: Leadership principle of creating environments for objective achievement
payload.justification: SG450 establishes leadership as a conceptual responsibility, representing a business principle.

payload.description: People engagement principle recognizing human capabilities as assets
payload.justification: SG451 identifies people engagement as a core business concept, representing a foundational principle.

payload.description: Process approach principle for interconnected activity management
payload.justification: SG452 establishes process approach as a conceptual model, representing a business principle.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making principle for quality management
payload.justification: SG454 establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule, representing a foundational principle.

payload.description: Relationship management principle for interdependent value creation
payload.justification: SG455 defines relationship management as a conceptual model, representing a business principle.

payload.description: Regulatory and legislative compliance constraints on organizational operations and policy scope
payload.justification: SG448 identifies regulatory compliance as a required element of the policy framework, representing a business constraint. | SG456 extends policy scope to include legislative responsibility requirements, representing a business constraint. | SG456 extends policy scope to include social responsibility requirements, representing a business constraint. | SG456 extends policy scope to include environmental responsibility requirements, representing a business constraint. | SG456 extends policy scope to include charitable responsibility requirements, representing a business constraint.

payload.description: Activities and resources are logically managed as processes or interconnected process series to achieve results
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a process approach principle establishing logical management philosophy at the Designer abstraction level

payload.description: QMS decisions are logically required to be based on analysis of data and information
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes an evidence-based decision making principle establishing logical requirement at the Designer abstraction level

payload.description: Temporal validity period attribute of policy authorization artifact
payload.justification: Signal indicates the signature block identifies temporal validity of the policy document. At Row 5, this represents a detailed data attribute specification — the validity period is a property of the authorization/policy entity. Moderate confidence as the Signal describes the temporal aspect indirectly.

payload.description: Director role with organizational policy approval authority
payload.justification: Signal describes a Director signature block representing specific organizational authority. At the Implementer level, this is a detailed role specification with defined approval authority. Classification as Role rather than Actor because the Signal emphasizes the authority function rather than a specific individual.

payload.description: Director signature requirement constraint for policy document validity
payload.justification: The signature block mechanism enforces a validation constraint — policy documents require Director-level authorization to be valid. This is a detailed rule governing document validity at the Implementer level. Confidence matches Signal confidence as this is a clear business rule inference.

payload.description: Establishes organizational quality policy aligned with business purpose and context as foundational governance framework.
Defines quality policy framework purpose for objective-setting and commitment to regulatory compliance and continuous improvement.
Articulates strategic commitment to understanding customer needs and exceeding expectations as organizational driver.
Expresses leadership commitment to creating enabling work environment for achieving organizational objectives.
Recognizes people engagement as essential organizational value and strategic enabler for business benefit.
Declares process approach as organizational efficiency principle for achieving desired results.
Establishes continual improvement across quality management as core annual organizational objective.
Commits organization to evidence-based decision making principle for quality management governance.
Recognizes mutually beneficial external provider relationships as strategic driver for value creation.
Declares policy commitment to social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibilities as organizational obligations.
Provides formal policy authorization by organizational director establishing governance authority and accountability.
Establishes the conceptual foundation for a quality policy aligned with organizational purpose and context.
Defines the policy framework as encompassing objective-setting, customer requirements, regulatory compliance, and continual improvement commitments.
Articulates customer focus as a business concept requiring understanding of needs, meeting requirements, and exceeding expectations.
Defines leadership as the conceptual responsibility for creating environments that enable objective achievement through people involvement.
Identifies people engagement as a core business concept recognizing human capabilities as essential organizational assets.
Establishes process approach as a conceptual model where activities and resources are managed as interconnected processes for efficiency.
Defines continual improvement as a central business objective across all quality management system aspects.
Establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule requiring data analysis before quality management system decisions.
Defines relationship management as a conceptual model of interdependent external provider relationships that mutually create value.
Extends policy scope to encompass social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibility requirements.
Process approach principle articulating logical management philosophy that activities and resources achieve results through process or interconnected process series.
Evidence-based decision making principle establishing logical requirement that QMS decisions follow data and information analysis.
Director signature block identifying specific organizational authority and temporal validity of policy document.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
